title: Derive Release 1215 from qualified Release 1214
description: Rebuild the exact Release 1214 source and permit only the reviewed five-path dam-mechanism delta.
close_reason: The dedicated stage tool reconstructs exact Release 1214, inventories it, and rejects changes outside 1dungeon, assistance, dam_mechanisms, shadow_logic, and zork1.

title: Make the dam panel physically readable
description: Add examination, diagnosis, listening, touch, and knock responses grounded in live interlock, gate, reservoir, and leak state.
close_reason: The control panel, bolt, and green bubble now expose real mechanism state without moving any canonical state themselves.

title: Explain the four maintenance buttons
description: Wrap the yellow, brown, red, and blue buttons while preserving their original state transitions and consequences.
close_reason: Button handlers explain the interlock, lights, and pipe circuit, then delegate PUSH and READ to canonical BUTTON-F.

title: Route tools into the real dam mechanisms
description: Support reasonable USE combinations with wrench, screwdriver, guidebook, water, panel, bolt, and leak.
close_reason: Dam-specific USE routing identifies valid interfaces and delegates valid wrench-and-bolt operation to canonical TURN.

title: Preserve canonical sluice and reservoir behavior
description: Keep the yellow interlock, wrench turn, gate toggle, and eight-turn fill or empty timing authoritative.
close_reason: The wrapper calls BOLT-F and records only actual GATES-OPEN transitions; I-RFILL and I-REMPTY are untouched.

title: Preserve the blue-button leak and real repair
description: Let players cause the canonical maintenance flood, reject wrong tools, and route real putty into FIX-MAINT-LEAK.
close_reason: Blue still runs BUTTON-F and I-MAINT-ROOM; USE PUTTY ON LEAK delegates to canonical PLUG and records only a real sealed WATER-LEVEL state.

title: Deepen MELZAR and persistent dam recap
description: Report interlock, gate, reservoir, and leak state through MELZAR and retain meaningful discoveries in RECAP.
close_reason: The learned diagnostic word now reports complete dam state, and RECAP records panel, tool, gate, light, leak, and repair outcomes.

title: Lock and publish Glulx Release 1215
description: Run pinned compile and native routes, commit deterministic identity, resolve review, and leave the stacked PR ready but unmerged.
close_reason: The 207,360-byte Release 1215 artifact is locked at checksum 0x3d135bb8 and SHA-256 ea23c8ff739348162f32c798ff0ad6f5e8e6a4d310ad3daf5c2da58b86505eed; the native mechanism route passed, the valid review finding was fixed and resolved, documentation is truthful, and stacked PR #7 remains open and unmerged.
